supply_chain.dependencies: Qualified vendors and contract manufacturers supply parts, services and product support. Some key parts may be obtained from only a qualified single supplier or a limited group of qualified suppliers.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from cybersecurity incidents, geopolitical tensions, trade restrictions, transportation interruptions, natural disasters, shortages of semiconductor components, limited availability of materials subject to environmental regulations

regulatory_risks: U.S. export regulations for semiconductor technology sold to China including wafer fabrication equipment and related parts and services
Export license requirements and Entity List restrictions limiting sales to certain Chinese customers
U.S. CHIPS and Science Act compliance requirements
Inflation Reduction Act 15% corporate minimum tax and 1% excise tax on stock repurchases
OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting Project global minimum tax regimes enacted in multiple countries
European Union Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
Environmental regulations on hazardous materials, discharge, recycling, transportation and disposal
Regulations limiting use of hydrofluorocarbons and per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
Data privacy and localization requirements in multiple jurisdictions
Trade compliance including import, export and customs regulations
Public procurement and public funding requirements
National security review requirements for foreign investments and acquisitions

sanction_risks: U.S. government export controls restricting sale of certain technologies to customers in China
Potential retaliatory measures from foreign governments
Conflict in the Middle East impacting operations
Russia's invasion of Ukraine creating heightened cybersecurity attack risk
Increasing tension with China creating heightened cybersecurity attack risk
